title: Studies on Copper(II) Complexes of Substituted Triazene-N-oxides. Estimation and Structure
author: K. B. SARAN
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Jadavpur University, Calcutta-700 032 

  Manuscript received 81 March 1983, revised 28 May 1984, accepted 15 February 1981  

 3-( o -Carboxyphenyl)-1-methyltriazene- N -oxide, 3-( m -acetophenyl)-1 -phenyltriazene­- N -oxide and 3-(p-acetophenyl)-1-phenyltriazene- N  oxide  react  with Cu ll  quantitatively in the pH ranges 1.0-7.5, 1.8-10.8 and 2.0-10.5, forming green Cu(C 8 H 7 O 3 N 2 ) 2  light yellow Cu(C 14 H 12 O 2 N 3 ) 2  and yellowish green Cu(C 14 H 12 O 2 N 3 ) 2  complexes, respec­tively. The complexes can be weighed as such after drying at 110-120°. Most of the cations and anions do not interfere. Determination of Cu ll  in ore and alloys has been   described. Magnetic susceptibility, it data and insolubility of the carboxytriazene Cu ll  complex in non-coordinating solvent suggest dimeric square planar structure of the complex. This reagent has been also used for the determination of Cu II  even in presence of cyanide.
